title: Calophyllum microphyllum
intro: Calophyllum microphyllum, also known as the small-leaf Calophyllum, is a species of tree in the family Calophyllaceae, native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Calophyllum microphyllum include calophyllic acid, calophylloic acid, and other flavonoids, which have been shown to possess potential therapeutic benefits. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include extracts, oils, and infusions, which are often used in traditional medicine and as a natural remedy for various health conditions.
